EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Substantial low level moisture combined with an upper level disturbance to produce showers and thunderstorms across Southern New England.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree on Hopkins Hollow Road was downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front moved northward through the area as an upper level disturbance moved across southern New England, resulting in isolated showers and thunderstorms.
Strong low pressure moving through New York State coupled with a vigorous low level jet resulted in periods of damaging wind across Southern New England. Rain and occasional thunder also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Automated Surface Observing System at T.F. Green State Airport (KPVD) recorded sustained winds of 44 mph and gusts to 56 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low pressure moving through New York State coupled with a vigorous low level jet resulted in periods of damaging wind across Southern New England. Rain and occasional thunder also occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Automated Surface Observing System at Westerly State Airport (KWST) recorded a wind gust to 49 mph.  In addition, several higher gusts were recorded on amateur radio operators' home weather stations.  Trees and branches were downed in Charlestown, Kingston, and North Kingstown, one branch landed on a car.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system in the Great Lakes redeveloped south of New England, spreading snow across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six to seven inches of snow fell across northwestern Providence County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system in the Great Lakes redeveloped south of New England, spreading snow across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six to seven inches of snow fell across southeastern Providence County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system in the Great Lakes redeveloped south of New England, spreading snow across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six to six and a half inches of snow fell across eastern Kent County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Bill developed into a tropical depression over the eastern Atlantic on August 15, 2009. Bill then strengthened into a hurricane over the next week, passing well to the east of Nantucket, August 22-23. While Bill passed Southern New England well offshore, he still made quite an impression on the area. Tropical moisture feeding into the area resulted in heavy rainfall which led to flooding and flash flooding. High swells churned up by Bill resulted in high surf and coastal flooding along portions of the Southern New England shoreline. These effects were felt the most by residents of Hillsborough County, New Hampshire, where five inches of rain fell in less than eight hours resulting in significant flooding across portions of the county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minor coastal flooding occurred in Narragansett at Scarborough Beach and in Charlestown.  Ocean waters reached the base of the boardwalk at Scarborough Beach.  In Charlestown, several driveways on Charlestown Beach Road were flooded with ocean waters.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: High surf flooded Galilee Beach in Narragansett up to the dunes with significant beach erosion occurring.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved across Southern New England into an unstable atmosphere, resulting in showers and thunderstorms forming along the front. Some of these storms became severe producing strong, damaging winds and frequent lightning strikes.||A group of nine people sought shelter under a tree at a sports field.  Lightning struck the ground or a fence nearby and the nine felt a shock from the strike.  Only minor injuries were sustained.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several streets in Cranston were flooded with water halfway up car tires (about 6 inches).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved across Southern New England into an unstable atmosphere, resulting in showers and thunderstorms forming along the front. Some of these storms became severe producing strong, damaging winds and frequent lightning strikes.||A group of nine people sought shelter under a tree at a sports field.  Lightning struck the ground or a fence nearby and the nine felt a shock from the strike.  Only minor injuries were sustained.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nine people, taking shelter under a tree at Foster Memorial Park, were injured when lightning struck nearby.  Five of the injured refused medical treatment, the other four were transported to a nearby hospital.  There were only minor injuries.  No information was available on their ages.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a house on North Lane, resulting in a house fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure off the mid-Atlantic coast intensified dramatically resulting in widespread snowfall along the northeast corridor of the U.S. While the mid-Atlantic received much of the snow and wind from this storm, snow spread across much of Southern New England and blizzard conditions occurred in Newport, Rhode Island and Marshfield, Massachusetts. Snowfall totals ranged from 1 to 3 inches in northwestern Massachusetts and southern New Hampshire to 18 to 20 inches across Rhode Island and southeastern Massachusetts. This resulted in numerous flight cancellations out of T.F. Green Airport in Providence and Logan Airport in Boston, school closings, and a struggle by plows to keep the roads clear.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Seventeen to twenty-one inches of snow fell across Newport County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A coastal low pressure system moved southeast of Nantucket, spreading snow across Southern New England. Snowfall amounts ranged from three inches on Cape Cod to almost twelve inches in southern New Hampshire. This late season storm affected most of the east coast and resulted in hundreds of flight cancellations at Boston's Logan Airport and many car accidents.  In Rhode Island, snowfall amounts ranged from seven to nearly twelve inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Seven to ten inches of snow fell across southeast Providence County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms developed across Southern New England as an upper level low moved across the area.  Weak low level winds resulted in these storms moving very slowly, if at all.  Rainfall rates of up to four inches per hour produced flash flooding, resulting in street closures and basement flooding at several locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large branches on Route 102 were downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A stationary front stretched from Hartford, Connecticut across northern Rhode Island to southeast Massachusetts combined with an upper level disturbance to produce showers and thunderstorms across southern New England.  Plenty of moisture was in place across these areas, increasing the chance for heavy rain and flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Portsmouth, a house on East Main Street was flooded with water going into the first floor of the house.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny size hail fell in the Wickford section of North Kingstown.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The intersection of Main and School Streets was flooded and other nearby streets were rendered impassable by flood waters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow moving front stretched south of Long Island moved northward, combining with an upper level disturbance to produce showers and thunderstorms across southern New England. Plenty of moisture was in place across these areas, increasing the chance for heavy rain and flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Atwood Avenue in Johnston and the intersection of Main and Charles Streets in North Providence were closed due to flooding.  Pawtucket Avenue at Waterman Avenue in Providence was impassable due to flooding.
